summary: GitHub’s article supports a bounded mechanism claim that cross-family review is inserted as an independent critique step, but it does not isolate which review checkpoint drives the reported benchmark gains.

bounded_claims.claim_text: GitHub introduced Rubber Duck as an experimental feature in GitHub Copilot CLI.
bounded_claims.bounds: This establishes feature introduction in experimental mode only; it does not support broader claims about general availability or performance effects.

bounded_claims.claim_text: Claude Sonnet 4.6 paired with Rubber Duck running GPT-5.4 closed 74.7% of the performance gap between Sonnet and Opus in GitHub’s SWE-Bench Pro evaluation.
bounded_claims.bounds: This is a vendor-reported benchmark result bounded to GitHub’s SWE-Bench Pro evaluation and does not by itself establish general performance gains outside that setting.

bounded_claims.claim_text: GitHub reports larger gains from Rubber Duck on harder tasks, including +3.8% on problems spanning 3 or more files and 70 or more steps and +4.8% on the hardest problems identified across three trials.
bounded_claims.bounds: These results support a scoped harder-task tendency claim, but they do not establish a universal benefit across task types and do not fully specify the hardest-problem selection method in the captured material.
